abstract: In service settings, chatbots frequently are associated with substandard care, depersonalization, and linguistic misunderstandings. Drawing on assemblage theory (i.e., the examination of how heterogeneous parts, through their ongoing interaction, create an emergent whole with new capacities that the parts themselves do not have), the authors investigate how chatbots’ language concreteness—the specificity of words used during interactions with consumers—can help improve satisfaction, willingness to use the chatbot, and perceived shopping efficiency. Across three experiments, the findings reveal a psychological mechanism driven by concrete chatbot language that makes chatbots seem competent and reinforces consumer self-competence, in turn boosting satisfaction, willingness to use the chatbot, and perceived shopping efficiency. This pattern of results contributes to consumer behavior by providing evidence of the chatbot language concreteness effect on consumer–chatbot interactions. For practitioners, the authors outline conversational designs that could help optimize implementation of chatbots in customer service.

ur.ai_reason: chatbot语言具体性影响消费者满意度、感知购物效率，3个实验，核心消费者心理
ur.tldr: 聊天机器人语言具体性对消费者满意度、使用意愿和购物效率的影响。研究通过三项实验表明，具体语言提升机器人能力感知并增强消费者自我效能感，从而改善满意度、使用意愿和感知购物效率。

title: How Chatbot Language Shapes Consumer Perceptions: The Role of Concreteness and Shared Competence
title_zh: Chatbot语言对消费者感知的影响：具体性与共同胜任力的作用
